historical_factor_attribution.caveat: Model-based attribution: beta times realized factor shock. This is not a pure realized causal decomposition.

heteroskedasticity_diagnostics.notes: Breusch-Pagan: auxiliary regression u_hat^2 on intercept and factor regressors; LM = T * R^2_aux; asymptotic chi-square(k_factors) under H0.

serial_correlation_diagnostics.notes: BG: auxiliary regression u_t on intercept, X_t, u_{t-1}..u_{t-p}; LM = T * R²_aux; asymptotic chi²(p) under H0. Same sample ordering as factor OLS.

factor_multicollinearity.assessment_ru: Низкая: типичные VIF и cond(R); коллинеарность не доминирует, но попарные корреляции всё равно учитывать.

heteroskedasticity_diagnostics.notes: Breusch-Pagan: auxiliary regression u_hat^2 on intercept and factor regressors; LM = T * R^2_aux; asymptotic chi-square(k_factors) under H0.

serial_correlation_diagnostics.notes: BG: auxiliary regression u_t on intercept, X_t, u_{t-1}..u_{t-p}; LM = T * R²_aux; asymptotic chi²(p) under H0. Same sample ordering as factor OLS.

factor_multicollinearity.assessment_ru: Низкая: типичные VIF и cond(R); коллинеарность не доминирует, но попарные корреляции всё равно учитывать.

axis_model.look_ahead_caveat: Look-ahead protection is a 1-month publication lag only. Release-date accurate vintage handling is out of scope for v1.

macro_regime_diagnostics.method_disclaimer: macro_two_axis_v1 is a diagnostic-only macro regime classifier. It does not affect optimizer weights, mandate gates, stress pass/fail, or weight release.

factor_regression.label: goldilocks
factor_regression.reason: n_obs below minimum threshold of 12 monthly observations

factor_regression.label: stagflation
factor_regression.reason: n_obs below minimum threshold of 12 monthly observations

factor_regression.label: recession_disinflation
factor_regression.reason: n_obs below minimum threshold of 12 monthly observations

stability_summary.warning: Stability threshold is a global heuristic, not factor-specific calibration.

neutral_transition.note: primary regime layer no longer assigns 'neutral_transition' as a final bucket; see transition_summary

episodes.note: Crisis window should show weak growth and stress-like inflation regime mix.
COVID shock should not be dominated by expansionary labels.
Inflation shock should show inflation-positive labels.
Tightening cycle should generally avoid broad risk-on reflation dominance.

portfolio_regime_analytics_window.label: 10Y
portfolio_regime_analytics_window.disclaimer: regime_label_history_span may be longer than the portfolio analytics slice; portfolio_regime_analytics_window is fixed to 10Y (~2520 trading days for daily regime_factor_analytics, or ~520 weeks / 120 months in legacy modes) ending at analysis_end.

regime_factor_analytics.portfolio_regime_analytics_note: macro_two_axis_v1 may emit regime labels over a longer history (regime_label_history_span) than the portfolio analytics slice; portfolio_regime_analytics_window is 10Y ending at analysis_end (daily: ~2520 trading days where available; weekly/monthly modes use their historical window lengths), aligned with the standard return/metrics/covariance/factor horizons.

goldilocks.label: goldilocks
warnings: Low confidence (~60–125 trading days); regime estimates not for optimization.

recession_disinflation.label: recession_disinflation
warnings: Low confidence (~60–125 trading days); regime estimates not for optimization.

collinearity_signal.basis: Oil/Commodity correlation and Oil VIF from extended diagnostic rows.

factor_variance_decomposition.residual_interpretation: The current factor model explains most weekly portfolio variance.
factor_variance_decomposition.residual_recommendation: Factor decomposition is suitable as a diagnostic risk-management signal.

frequency_disclosure.macro_regime_frequency_notes: Portfolio metrics and covariance use returns_frequency=daily; factor betas/regression/stress shocks stay weekly; macro regime classifier labels remain monthly; regime_factor_analytics may use daily returns when daily data loads (see regime_factor_analytics.summary).
